'''Joel Monaghan''' (born 22 April 1982) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. An [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian international]] and [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] representative {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er, he previously played in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for the [[Canberra Raiders]] and the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Joel Monaghan was born in [[Canberra]], Australian Capital Territory, Australia. Monaghan's junior development was influenced by [[National Rugby League]] coach [[Neil Henry]] who was Monaghan's coach at [[Erindale College, Canberra]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2008}} While attending Erindale College, Monoghan played for the [[Australia national schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys team]] in 2000.<ref>{{cite web|title=SportingPulse Homepage for Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|url=http://www.sportingpulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|access-date=2008-10-10|publisher=SportingPulse|archive-date=31 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170131030043/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=7-2130-0-0-0&sID=26424|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He is the younger brother of fellow rugby league player [[Michael Monaghan]].

==Playing career==
[[File:JoelMonaghan.jpg|thumb|left|Monaghan playing for the Raiders in 2008]]

===National Rugby League===
Monaghan made his NRL début in 2001 for Canberra against the [[Penrith Panthers]] in round 10 and spent four seasons with the club.

He debuted for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in round 2005, against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]. He missed much of the 2006 season after injuring his knee in a trail match against the [[St George Dragons]] then suffering later hamstring injuries during the year. He had a successful 2007 season at the Roosters scoring 13 tries in 17 games.

In 2008, Monaghan returned to the Canberra Raiders for the 2008 season.<ref name="Monaghan returns to Raiders">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/m/monaghan_joel/index.php|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060426072719/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/m/monaghan_joel/index.php|url-status=dead|archive-date=26 April 2006|title=Monaghan returns to Raiders|publisher=rleague.com|date=31 December 2006|access-date=1 January 2007}}</ref> He won the [[Mal Meninga Medal]] for the club's ''Player of the Year''.<ref>{{cite web|work=[[The Canberra Times]] |title=Monaghan Raiders' finest |author=Peter Fitzgerald |url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/monaghan-raiders-finest/1269670.aspx |date=2008-09-11 |access-date=2008-09-11 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110716170258/http://www.canberratimes.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/monaghan-raiders-finest/1269670.aspx |archive-date=16 July 2011 }}</ref> In August 2008, Monaghan was named in the [[Australia national rugby league team#Rugby League World Cup Squad|preliminary 46-man Kangaroos squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref>{{cite news|author=Liam FitzGibbon |publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia) |title=Surprises in Kangaroos squad |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24111780-23214,00.html |access-date=2008-08-02 |date=2008-08-01 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080808144730/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0%2C8659%2C24111780-23214%2C00.html |archive-date= 8 August 2008 }}</ref><ref name="Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.iht.com/articles/ap/2008/08/01/sports/AS-RGL-Australia-Squad.php|title=Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad|publisher=International Herald Tribune|date=2008-08-01|access-date=2008-08-02}}</ref> and in October 2008 he was selected in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Australia|Australia]] squad.<ref name="Manly six win Australia call-up">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656211.stm|title=Manly six win Australia call-up|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref> He made his test début on the {{rlp|wg}} for Australia's opening game of the RLWC against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]] on Sunday, 26 October 2008. In the final, he made the mistake of tackling [[Lance Hohaia]] who was without the ball, which resulted in a penalty try, changing a two-point deficit to an 8-point deficit with only 10 minutes left.<ref>{{cite news| url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7740369.stm| publisher=BBC News| title=Live text – Rugby League World Cup final| date=2008-11-22| access-date=2010-05-06| first=Pranav| last=Soneji}}</ref>

After the end of the 2010 season, Monaghan was released from his contract with the Raiders at his request.<ref name="Joel Monaghan in tears after quitting the Raiders">{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/joel-monaghan-in-tears-after-quitting-the-raiders-20101109-17lff.html|title=Joel Monaghan in tears after quitting the Raiders|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|date=9 November 2010|access-date=1 January 2011}}</ref> His decision to leave the Raiders came after a photograph of him simulating a lewd act with a dog during [[Mad Monday]] celebrations was published on [[Twitter]].<ref name="Monaghan faces sack over Mad Monday dog photo disgrace">{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/monaghan-faces-sack-over-mad-monday-dog-photo-disgrace-20101104-17fx1.html|last=Walter|first=Brad|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]|title=Monaghan faces sack over Mad Monday dog photo disgrace|date=4 November 2010|access-date=1 January 2011}}</ref> The photograph was noted and discussed by media outlets around the world.<ref name=ScandalGoesGlobal>[http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-sport/monaghan-dog-picture-scandal-goes-global-20101105-17hcy.html "Monaghan dog picture scandal goes global"], Glenn Cullen, ''[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]'', 5 November 2010</ref>

===Super League===
On 3 December 2010 it was announced that English [[Super League]] club [[Warrington Wolves]] had signed Monaghan on a one-year contract.<ref>{{Cite news 
| title = Warrington Wolves sign Joel Monaghan on one-year deal
| publisher = BBC Sport
| place = UK
| date = 3 December 2010
| url = https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/warrington/9256331.stm
| access-date = 4 December 2010}}</ref> There he joined his brother [[Michael Monaghan]].

Monaghan was selected for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] squad for the [[International Origin]] match against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]] on 10 June 2011.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|title=Brian McClennan completes Exiles squad to face England fixture|work=BBC Sport|date=4 May 2011|archive-date=13 January 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160113141331/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/13278410|url-status=live}}</ref> However, he was not selected to play.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2011-06-08 |title=George Carmont snatches Exiles victory at Headingley |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/13696752 |access-date=2024-04-22 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref>

Monaghan was selected for the 2012 Exiles to face [[England national rugby league team|England]] by new coach [[Daniel Anderson (rugby league)|Daniel Anderson]], he started on the {{rlp|wg}} in Game 1.

He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=The Guardian|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
}}</ref>

He played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|publisher=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/>

Monaghan was selected to play for the [[Warrington Wolves]] on the {{rlp|wg}} in the [[2013 Super League Grand final]] and scored the opening try in their loss against the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |access-date=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|publisher=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=The Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref> He was the regular season's top try-scorer in 2014.

Castleford Tigers confirmed Monaghan's signing in October 2015.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34637507|title=Joel Monaghan: Castleford Tigers sign former Warrington captain|date=2015-10-27|access-date=2019-06-27|language=en-GB}}</ref> He joined the club on a two-year deal, seemingly in anticipation of Justin Carney's departure.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.eurosport.com/rugby-league/castleford-sign-joel-monaghan-from-warrington_sto4967420/story.shtml|title=Castleford sign Joel Monaghan from Warrington|date=2015-10-27|website=Eurosport|access-date=2019-06-27}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.totalrl.com/salford-land-carney-for-2016/|title=Salford land Carney for 2016|last=Bower|first=Aaron|date=2015-11-03|website=Total Rugby League|language=en-US|access-date=2019-06-27}}</ref> Across his two seasons with the Tigers, Monaghan made 34 appearances and scored 13 tries.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org" />
